observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make an extensive review and evaluation of the accounting records to determine the causes of the abnormal balances of the liabilities and draw Journal Entry Voucher to correct the deficiency.
observations.managementAction: No actions were taken by the management. The evaluation of the accounting records on the account is still in progress as of year-end. Currently, no additional errors have been found in the subsidiary ledgers that would reduce the identified abnormal balance, as the majority of the transactions occurred prior to the current Municipal Accountant’s tenure.
observations.reason: Other Payables account still showed a negative balance of P645,642.59, under the General Fund, as of December 31, 2024. According to the Municipal Accountant, the evaluation of the accounting records on the account is still in progress as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to continuously reconcile the discrepancy of P289,743.75 between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts by reviewing all relevant transactions and accounting entries affecting the accounts and make the necessary adjustments to bring both balances in agreement.
observations.managementAction: No actions were taken by the management.
observations.reason: The discrepancy between the reciprocal accounts increased to P807,250.73 due to recording errors, which the Municipal Accountant plans to adjust within CY 2025. As of year-end, no additional recording errors have been identified, as most of the affected transactions occurred prior to the current Municipal Accountant’s tenure.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Officials to: a. Design or adopt strategies to deal with the increasing expenditures in the operation of the economic enterprises; and b. Develop measures that will improve the ability of the LEEs to generate adequate income to sustain its operations. Also, consider revisiting and updating the Municipality’s Revenue Code to adjust the current imposed rates to meet the current operating costs of the economic enterprises.
observations.managementAction: No actions were taken by the management.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2024, the LEEs of the Municipality continued to operate at a net loss. A proposed revision to the Municipality’s Revenue Code has already been submitted and is currently awaiting approval. In addition, an increase in the public market rates is planned to be implemented upon completion of its renovation/construction.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts to
properly recognize the construction of the access road to the demo farm, drainage
canals, and the fencing and pavement of the evacuation center under their
respective PPE accounts. Likewise, capitalize the costs incurred for major repairs
and rehabilitation of various local roads by appropriately adding them to the cost
of the related existing PPE accounts; and
b. Ensure that all future construction projects, as well as major repair and
rehabilitation activities, are properly recorded and capitalized under the relevant
PPE accounts, in accordance with the recognition and measurement criteria set
forth in IPSAS 17.

Moreover, we recommended that the MGSO conduct a physical inspection of the
aforementioned infrastructure projects to verify their existence and ensure that all
verified properties are adequately documented and included in their property
records and the RPCPPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to use
the appropriate accounts in recording transactions, in accordance with Annex B of
COA Circular No. 2015-009, to avoid account misclassification and ensure the fair
presentation of the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MGSO to:
a. Ensure that all necessary documents, including the deed of donation, are obtained
from the donor prior to accepting and taking possession of any donated assets to
ensure that the asset is legally and properly transferred to the Municipality, for
accurate recognition;
b. Include the donated PTV in the inventory records and regularly update the
inventory records to reflect the receipt of donated assets, ensuring that these
assets are properly documented and accurately reported in the RPCPPE; and
c. Furnish the Municipal Accountant with copies of the deed of donation for the
donated PTV and any subsequent donated properties to ensure timely recording
and proper recognition of assets.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to record the donated PTV by debiting Motor Vehicles account by
P2,120,000.00 and crediting Prior Period Adjustments account by P1,904,214.29 and
Accumulated Depreciation account by P215,785.71 in the books of account upon
receipt of the required supporting documents, following Item 4.2 of COA Circular No.
2020-006 and Paragraphs 26 and 27 of IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
continuously reconcile the discrepancy of P807,250.73 between the Due from Other
Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts by reviewing all relevant transactions and
accounting entries, and making the required adjustments to reconcile the balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
perform a comprehensive and continuous review of the accounting records to
determine the causes of the abnormal balances of the account and to prepare
Journal Entry Vouchers to correct any identified deficiencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Review the advance payment adjustments and retrieve all relevant documents
related to previous amortization payments to ensure that the recorded loan
balances are properly verified and accurately reflect the Municipality’s actual loan
obligations; and
b. Prepare the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to adjust the loan balances
based on the bank confirmations provided by the Land Bank’s Loan Operations
Field Unit which should be supported by separate subsidiary ledgers maintained
for each loan, to facilitate accurate tracking, reliable reconciliation, and full
compliance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare Schedule of Unreleased Checks monthly and
forward the same to the Municipal Accountant, in compliance with Item 2.3 of COA
GAFMIS Circular Letter No. 2002-001; and
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare a JEV at year-end to record the restoration of
unreleased checks and recognize the appropriate liability accounts to reflect the
correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements, in
compliance with Item 2.4 of COA GAFMIS Circular Letter No. 2002-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Secure all relevant information and supporting documents for the Due to NGAs and
Due to GOCCs accounts totaling P270,107.03 and P544,491.38, respectively, in
accordance with Section 111 of PD No. 1445 to facilitate reconciliation and
validation of these accounts;
b. Conduct regular verification, analysis, and validation of all inter-agency fund
transfer accounts to assess their existence, validity, and transaction dates to
determine whether any balances qualify as dormant accounts that may need to be
disposed of, in accordance with Item 7.1 of COA Circular No. 2023-008 or make
necessary adjustments based on these processes, including the review and
correction of any potential misclassification of liabilities;
c. Confirm the balances of the Due to NGAs and Due to GOCCs accounts with the
respective agencies, including GSIS, DepEd, and any other relevant entities
identified during the validation process by sending a formal confirmation letter to
ensure the accuracy of the reported balances and to document any additional
efforts made by the municipality; and
d. Maintain the general ledgers and subsidiary ledgers in the prescribed formats and
update them in real-time, in compliance with Section 114 of PD No. 1445 and
Annexes 5 and 6 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Chapter 1, Volume 2. This should be
done while addressing the ongoing reconciliation of reciprocal accounts, verifying
unaccounted balances, and completing the transition to an electronic accounting
system to support proper record-keeping and ensure timely updates of transaction
records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Officials to develop
measures that will improve the ability of the LEEs to generate adequate income to
sustain its operations. Also, prioritize the finalization and approval of the revised
Revenue Code of the Municipality to adjust the current imposed rates therein to meet
the current operating costs of the economic enterprises.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Coordinate with the BLGF and DICT, in collaboration with the Municipal
Assessor’s Office, to facilitate the automation of the RPT system, as the
Municipality qualifies for DICT’s support, it should fully utilize this assistance to
enhance data accuracy and ensure proper reconciliation;
b. Request sufficient budget allocation from the Municipal Budget Officer for the
implementation of the RPT administration operation automation system, ensuring
the availability of sufficient resources to support the system’s operation and any
potential additional needs; and
c. Furnish the Municipal Accountant with a copy of the duly signed certified list of
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible at the beginning of each year to
prevent reliance on the summary of real property assessment.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant establish the RPT and SET
receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of taxpayers
provided by the Municipal Treasurer, ensuring accurate recording, in compliance with
Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Chapter 3, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MGSO to:
a. Assign a unique property numbers to all PPE items in accordance with Item 5.6 of
COA Circular No. 2020-006 to ensure accurate identification and accountability of
assets; and
b. Affix property stickers containing the vital information specified in Item 5.7 of the
same COA Circular during the inventory-taking process and ensure that the stickers
are placed in a prominent yet secured location on the property for easy and
immediate identification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services
Officer (MGSO) to ensure that all POs are completed with the necessary information as
outlined in Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010, instead of merely copying the data
provided in the PRs, to facilitate thorough evaluation and transparency in the
procurement process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the HRMO to prepare a contract,
MOA, or job order between the Municipality of Liliw and each JO worker to establish a
clear and binding agreement and ensure that comprehensive provisions, including
but not limited to the workers’ duties, responsibilities, terms of employment, payment
structure, and the agreed method of payment (daily wage or piece of work) were
stated in the contract to facilitate the proper validation of workers performance and
accuracy of payments.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to provide the required disclosure for the Intangible Asset Account in accordance with Section 16 of the MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already provided the required disclosure for the intangible assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to: a. Facilitate the preparation and submission of the duly accomplished Property Inventory Form (PIF) considering the buildings and other structures owned by the Municipality to the Office of the Auditor and the GIF of GSIS on or before April 30 of each year to be used as basis for the assessment of the general insurance coverage; and b. Ensure that insurable properties especially the Municipal Building are covered by property insurance as required under RA No. 656, as amended by COA Circular No. 92-930.
observations.managementAction: The duly accomplished PIF including the buildings and other structures owned by the Municipality were prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor and the GIF of GSIS within the prescribed period. The Municipal Building was covered by property insurance in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MGSO to ensure that copies of approved POs together with their supporting documents are submitted within five working days from their execution or issuance, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Copies of approved POs, along with their supporting documents, are submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five working days from their execution or issuance, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Secretariat to post in the PhilGEPS website the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed within three calendar days and 15 calendar days from the date of issuance, respectively, for the procurements done thru public bidding and all instances of alternative methods of procurement, except for contracts with Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) of P50,000.00 and below; and b. The Municipal Accountant to ensure that the printout copy of the postings of the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed in the PhilGEPS website is included among the supporting documents for payment of completed infrastructure projects and procured goods and services.
observations.managementAction: Printouts of the PhilGEPS postings for the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed were attached among the supporting documents for payment of completed infrastructure projects and procured goods and services. The Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed for all procurement activities of the Municipality were posted on the PhilGEPS website within three (3) calendar days and 15 calendar days, respectively, from the date of issuance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions by promoting deserving employees among the present pool of manpower resources with exemplary performance and hiring new employees for lower-level positions to further improve services, provide equal job opportunities to the community and attain the full implementation of programs, plans and activities which will in turn benefit the Municipality as a whole. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to review the annual budget of personnel and revise the schedules for the succeeding years.
observations.managementAction: The filling of vacant positions has been prioritized by the Municipality for CY 2024. The annual budget allocation for personnel services has been thoroughly reviewed and revised.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to: a. Ensure that the regular functions are performed by the regular plantilla personnel of the regular Municipality who could be held responsible and accountable for their actions; and b. Evaluate the JOs and CoS workers assigned in various departments/offices and determine if some would be qualified to be hired as regular employees and/or elevated to casual status.
observations.managementAction: The majority of regular functions were carried out by Plantilla or regular personnel during CY 2024. The HRMO has coordinated with relevant offices to evaluate the performance of JO/CoS workers and give priority in hiring or promotion to regular or casual status during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the officials concerned to: a. Submit to the Audit Team valid justification and/or legal basis for charging the noted transactions to the 20 percent DF and refrain from using the fund to various PPAs which are not in accordance with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020; and b. Ensure the implementation of all the planned PPAs included in the AIP of the 20 percent DF to attain the optimal utilization of the fund and achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: A valid justification for charging the noted transactions to the 20 percent DF totaling P1,653,246.75 was submitted to the Audit Team. Out of the total appropriation of P30,057,230.40, a total amount of P28,476,601.40 or 94.74 percent was utilized as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the HRMO to coordinate with the GSIS to update the payroll of employees in the GSIS system. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor remit the outstanding balance of P155,998.97 and ensure that all the amount withheld are remitted intact within the first 10 days of the calendar month following the month to which the contributions apply.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO has already coordinated with the GSIS to update the payroll of employees in the GSIS system. The Municipal Treasurer already remitted the outstanding balance of P155,998.97.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to review all transactions and accounting entries that affect the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to determine the cause of the discrepancy and prepare necessary adjusting entries to bring both balances in agreement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has reviewed the transactions and accounting entries affecting the accounts.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still in progress. For CY 2023, a discrepancy of P289,743.75 was still noted between the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds. Reiterated in Part II, Audit Observation No. 3, Page Nos. 28 to 29.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions by promoting deserving employees among the present pool of manpower resources with exemplary performance, allowing the entry of new applicants for lower-level positions, in compliance with the existing guidelines and rules and regulations of the Civil Service Commission (CSC) to attain full implementation of the program, plans, and activities which will in turn benefit the Municipality as a whole. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to review the annual budget of personnel schedules for the succeeding years.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The allocated budget for the vacant department head positions were still lower than the authorized budget due to insufficient financial resource. Reiterated in Part II, Audit Observation No. 8, Page Nos. 36 to 38.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to transfer all the unexpended balances of LDRRMF to the Trust Liabilities - DRRMF account and to properly disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in the NTFS, pursuant to Sections 5.1.12 and 5.1.16 of the COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has transferred all the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account. Moreover, the details of the unexpended balances were properly disclosed in the NTFS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to extensively plan the projects that should be included in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and ensure that these projects are feasible to be implemented within the year.
observations.managementAction: Out of the total appropriations, a total amount of P26,382,658.28 or 93.26 percent was utilized by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to: a. ensure that all the PPAs included in the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) will be implemented during the year so that the envisioned GAD objectives and results will be fully achieved; and b. furnish the Audit Team with a copy of GAD AR within five working days from the end of January of the ensuing year, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001.
observations.managementAction: Out of the total appropriation of P7,719,432.45 for CY 2023, a total amount of P7,374,120.32 or 95.53 percent was expended by the Municipality. A copy of GAD AR was submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed deadline.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
review all transactions and accounting entries that affect the Due from Other
Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and determine the cause of the
discrepancy. Likewise, make the necessary adjusting entries to bring both
balances in agreement, thus ensuring the reliability of the reciprocal accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MGSO and the Municipal
Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records and make the
necessary corrections or adjustments to the discrepancies noted between their
records.

We also recommend that the MGSO ensure the submission of the required
RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year as prescribed under Section 124
of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to make sure that the Cash in Bank balances in the
Statement of Financial Position should tally with the balances in the Bank
Reconciliation Statements and cashbooks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
provide necessary disclosures on the unexpended balance of LDRRMF totaling
P1,748,237.58 in the Notes to Financial Statements as required in Sections 5.1.16
of the COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
exhaust all possible actions to collect the amount due and the Municipal
Accountant to set up the allowance for impairment for accounts receivables and
initiate/cause the verification of the nature of transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal officials to
design or adopt measures and strategies to remedy the high expenditures in the
operation of the economic enterprises. Also, revisit and update the
Municipality’s Revenue Code to adjust the tax rates therein to meet the current
operating costs of these economic enterprises.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDC to ensure the
implementation of all the planned PPAs included in the Annual Investment Plan
(AIP) of the 20 percent Development Fund to attain the optimal utilization of the
fund and to help achieve the desired socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) to monitor the available budget
and utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
(LDRRMF) to effectively implement the planned PPAs within the targeted
schedule and to ensure the optimal utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials to
examine the completeness of the required supporting documents as well as the
accuracy of future claims to avoid audit suspensions or disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
and other concerned officials to submit the required lacking documents and
ensure that subsequent payments are properly supported with complete
documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials to stop
the payment of cash for regular expenses, subject to reimbursement. These
payments should be made in checks paid directly to the suppliers as prescribed in
COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person
to:
a. ensure that all the PPAs included in the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) will be
implemented during the year so that the envisioned GAD objectives and
results will be fully achieved; and
b. furnish the Audit Team with a copy of GAD AR within five working days from
the end of January of the ensuing year, in compliance with COA Circular No.
2014-001; and
c. submit the GAD AR together with the required attachments as provided under
Section 4.1.C.8 PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended, as
follows:
1. summary of the reported program or project;
2. copies of reported policy issuances;
3. results of HGDG tests; and
4. actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and
recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO)
to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records and make the necessary
adjustments on both records, if warranted, to reflect the correct balance of the
PPE accounts;
b. ICO-MGSO to complete the conduct of physical count of PPEs of the
Municipality and update the required report thereon;
c. Municipal Accountant to properly identify the composition of each PPE
accounts reported in lumpsum to make the necessary adjustment and
properly reconcile the PPEs reflected in both records;
d. ICO-MGSO to prepare RPCPPE using the prescribed format separating the
PPE accounts with semi-expendable assets and submit to the Office of the
Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; and
e. Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to drop the semi-
expendable assets from the PPE accounts and ensure that those items are
covered by ICS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the ICO-MGSO to prepare and submit a Report on Local Road Network
(RLRN);
b. require the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the Report on the
Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN);
c. request the Municipal Engineer to provide the ICO-MGSO and Municipal
Accountant the complete description and cost segregation of road components
for road projects as required under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November
25, 2015; and
d. Municipal Accountant to update its existing Subsidiary Ledgers showing the
details of each road network of the Municipality and comply with the disclosure
requirement/format for the road network system.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant:
a. to expedite the analysis and recording of long outstanding reconciling items
to their appropriate accounts;
b. take up the reconciling items in the books immediately after the preparation of
the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements to avoid accumulated
adjustments;
c. immediately communicate bank errors with bank officials as soon as such
errors are discovered; and
d. review, evaluate and reconcile the Cash in Bank balances presented in the
Statement of Financial Position and the Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the difference
between the accounts Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education
Tax Receivables and their contra accounts; and
b. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments, furnishing the Office
of the Auditor with a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking into
account the adjustments made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

ICO-Municipal General Services Officer
a. to finalize the list of unserviceable properties and continue tracing the required
documents necessary for its disposal;
b. to check the condition of the property and record any damage, obsolete and
unserviceable property in the IIRUP;
c. to prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor the request for disposal of all
unserviceable property together with the IIRUP, Appraisal Report and other
required documents;
Appraisal and Disposal Committee
d. to undertake the appraisal of the unserviceable properties and initiate the
immediate disposal of these properties as required in the rules and procedures
provided in COA Circular No. 92-386; and
Municipal Accountant
e. To draw an appropriate Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop the unserviceable
properties from the books of accounts of the Municipality upon disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
review all transactions and accounting entries that affect the Due from Other
Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and determine the cause of the
discrepancy. Likewise, make the necessary adjusting entries to bring both
balances in agreement, thus ensuring the reliability of the reciprocal accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and ICO-MGSO to strictly adopt the Perpetual
Inventory Method in accounting for procured inventory items; and
b. ICO-MGSO to ensure the conduct of a complete physical count of inventories
and the periodic reconciliation with the accounting records to facilitate timely
verification and adjustment of the discrepancy, if there is any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
make an extensive review and evaluation of the accounting records to determine
the causes of the abnormal balances and draw Journal Entry Voucher to correct
the deficiency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/ Deficit and
disclose the said reconciliation in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance
with Section 29 of the IPSAS No. 2.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the amount of unexpended balance of the LDRRMF
between the Utilization Report and the Trust Liability – DRRMF account
reflected in the Statement of Financial Position;
b. upon reconciliation of the unexpended balances, direct the Municipal
Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the recorded balance
of Trust Liability-DRRMF account under Trust Fund; and
c. disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in
the Notes to Financial Statement pursuant to Sections 5.1.12 and 5.1.16 of the
COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council to:
a. update and improve the existing Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Plan of the Municipality by focusing on the programs, projects
and activities requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and impact on
the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management;

observations.reason: There still remain some stale checks which were not canceled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make an extensive review of the accounting records to determine the cause/causes of the abnormal account balances and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2018, the asset, liability and equity accounts with negative balances were reduced to P99.04, P748,499.26 and P316,907.71, respectively.
observations.reason: There still remain some asset, liability and equity accounts which were presented with negative balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the regular and timely submission of paid disbursement vouchers and supporting documents as required in Section 7.2.1a of the RRSA prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to submit the documents.
observations.reason: Though the unsubmitted 2017 DVs were submitted during the year, several 2018 DVs involving the amount of P642,440.00 were still not submitted within the required time.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned to ensure that the detailed engineering is made before the preparation of the Program of Work to warrant the timely implementation of all infrastructure projects. We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) not to proceed with the bidding of infrastructure contracts unless the Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned have conducted and completed the detailed engineering of the projects to be implemented and to take appropriate actions against those responsible in the conduct of bidding despite non-completion of the detailed engineering.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared the Detailed Engineering. Most of the projects implemented during the early months of CY 2018 were implemented in the later part of CY 2018 after the Detailed Engineering and other officials concerned completed the Detailed Engineering.
observations.reason: Although detailed engineering was not conducted for projects implemented during the early months of CY 2018, projects implemented in the later months of CY 2018 were carried out only after the Detailed Engineering of the projects was completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned to allocate the estimated cost for laboratory tests in the Program of Works, Plans and Designs to ensure that the allocation for the testing of materials to be used in the infrastructure projects is included in the ABC.
observations.managementAction: Management has allocated the cost of laboratory test for projects implemented in the later part of CY 2018.
observations.reason: Although a substantial amount of the costs for laboratory tests was integrated in the estimated costs presented in the Program of Works prepared for projects implemented in the later months of CY 2018, none was included in the Program of Works prepared in the early months of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Secretariat to post Notices of Award, and Notices to Proceed and copies of contracts within the period required in Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184, to promote transparency and accountability in the procurement process and in the implementation of the contract. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor take appropriate action against those responsible for non-compliance with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had instructed the officials concerned to comply fully with the requirements of Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.reason: The Municipal Mayor instructed the officials concerned and the Notices of Award, and Notices to Proceed and copies of contracts were posted in PhilGEPS in accordance with the R-IRR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the officials concerned to ensure that documentation of the disbursements involving the supplemental feeding funds are all submitted and ensure the integrity of information between the documents.
observations.managementAction: All the documents pertaining to the supplemental feeding conducted in CY 2018 were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate actions on the deficiencies noted and ensure that henceforth, payments pertaining to the pension program of the DSWD shall be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The deficiencies noted in the documentation of the Pension Program were corrected/adjusted by the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer. In CY 2018, payments made in relation to the Pension Program were supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer that collections be reported on the day they were actually received and be recognized as income, in compliance with Section 19 of the MNGAS for the LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Collections made in CY 2018 were reported on the same day they were actually received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor regularly monitor and evaluate the significance of the approved programs, projects and activities to the current needs of the constituents, so that the PPAs that have become less important and/or no longer responsive may be reprogrammed.
observations.managementAction: Management has implemented only 66 percent of its PPAs under the 20 DF in CY 2018. PPAs which remained responsive to the needs of the constituents were implemented. Reprogramming of projects which were no longer responsive was undertaken.
observations.reason: Although a substantial amount of the unexpended balances of the 20 percent DF was reprogrammed to projects responsive to the current needs of the constituents, several projects/programs remained unimplemented as of year-end. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 17, Page No. 52.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned Municipal Officials to ensure that the LDRRMF are utilized immediately for the acquisition of the needed equipment and tools and the implementation of projects to upgrade the Municipality’s disaster resiliency level and protect the vulnerable populace from exposure to the impacts of hazards and disasters.
observations.managementAction: Management has reprogrammed the LDRRMF in CY 2018 to be utilized for the acquisition of equipment and the improvement of facilities to upgrade the Municipality’s disaster resiliency level.
observations.reason: The unexpended LDRRMF were not utilized during the year as it was reprogrammed to new programs/projects which demanded amounts more than the available LDRRMF. Hence, implementation of the projects was deferred until the following year wherein the 2018 LDRRMF balance was expected to fill-in the difference. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 15, Page No. 48.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require GAD Focal Point System and other Municipal officials concerned to ensure that GAD programs/activities are responsive to the promotion of women empowerment, gender equality and removal of bias among genders.
observations.managementAction: Management has implemented GAD programs and projects for the GAD. Management has taken actions to make the GAD programs responsive to the needs of women for empowerment.
observations.reason: Not all GAD programs and activities implemented during the year were responsive to the needs of women to be empowered. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 21, Page No. 57.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an Ordinance that would provide the official guidelines in the granting of medical/financial assistance to qualified beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: No Ordinance, that would provide the official guidelines in the granting of medical/financial assistance to beneficiaries was enacted by the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality.

observations.managementAction: Depreciation expenses presented in the financial statements already tally with those recorded in the subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances reported on December 31, 2016 and henceforth, observe strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Eighty-one percent (81%) or P57,871.56 of the P71,071.56 reported on December 31, 2016 were liquidated by concerned accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to monitor regularly the status of checks issued and accordingly prepare Journal Entry Vouchers adjusting the amount of stale checks in the books of accounts following the procedures stated under Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: As of November 30, 2017, there were already 33 stale checks totaling P382,242.72 that were not cancelled yet by the Municipality.

observations.managementAction: Depreciation? (table text indicates reconciliation efforts) The 2016 balances of Due From Other Funds and Due to Other Funds were not reconciled. As of December 31, 2017, the difference was increased to P7,114,123.82.

observations.recommendation: We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required financial information in the Notes to Financial Statement to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with additional information value.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to implement the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
a. Bidding for the procurement of infrastructure projects was undertaken despite the non-completion of the Detailed Engineering, contrary to Section 17.6 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned to ensure that the detailed engineering is made before the preparation of the Program of Work to warrant timely implementation of all infrastructure projects. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) not to proceed with the bidding of infrastructure contracts unless the Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned have conducted and completed the detailed engineering of the projects to be implemented and to take appropriate actions against those responsible in the conduct of bidding despite non-completion of the detailed engineering.
b. The allocation of the cost of the laboratory test on the quality of materials was not included in the preparation of the Program of Work (POW), thus, was likewise not considered in the preparation of the Approved Budget for Contract (ABC), contrary to Section B.3 of DPWH DO No.22, series of 2015 and the implied requirement of Section 11.1 of Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned to allocate the estimated cost for laboratory tests in the Program of Works, Plans and Designs to ensure that the allocation for the testing of materials to be used in the infrastructure projects are included in the ABC.
c. The invitation or request for submission of price quotations for Shopping under Sections 52.1 (b), Negotiated Procurement under Sections 53.1 (two-failed biddings) and 53.9 (small value procurement) were not posted in the PhilGEPS, the website of the procuring entity, if any, or in any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality, contrary to Section 54.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct BAC officials to post the Requests for Price Quotations and Notices of Award in the PhilGEPS or in the website of the agency, if any, and any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor take appropriate actions against those responsible for non-compliance.
observations.managementAction:
a. The presiding BAC Chairman assured the Audit Team that bidding for infrastructure contracts shall be made only after the detailed engineering activities have been sufficiently carried out and approved by the Municipal Mayor.
b. The Management committed to implement the recommendation.
c. The BAC Secretariat assured the Audit Team of compliance with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation:
a.) Notices of Award were issued several days ahead of the Municipal Mayor’s approval of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Resolutions Declaring the Lowest Calculated and Responsive Bid (LCRB) and Recommending Approval for projects, contrary to Sections 37.1.1, 37.1.2 and 37.1.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, thus casted doubt on the regularity and propriety of the transactions. We recommended that the BAC and other concerned Municipal Officials submit a written justification/explanation why proper and normal contract-awarding procedures were not followed in the implementation of the aforementioned infrastructure projects.
b.) Notices of Award, Notices to Proceed and approved contracts were not posted in the PhilGEPS or in the website of the Municipality, contrary to Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, to promote transparency and accountability in the procurement process and in the implementation of the contract. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Secretariat to post Notices of Award, Notices to Proceed and approved contracts within the period required in Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, and to take appropriate action against those responsible for non-compliance.
observations.managementAction: We recommended that the BAC and other concerned Municipal Officials submit a written justification/explanation why proper and normal contract-awarding procedures were not followed in the implementation of the aforementioned infrastructure projects. The Municipal Officials concerned submitted justification/explanation. Notices of Award were issued only after approval of the pertinent BAC Secretary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWDO and other concerned officials require that applicants submit complete documentary evidence before issuing Certifications of Eligibility and that contractors' and suppliers' claims be processed, approved and paid only when supported by complete documentation as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Most of the required documents were not yet submitted by the Municipality. Some documentary evidence such as test results and the documentary evidence of the detailed engineering activities conducted were not attached to the 2017 DVs submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint somebody to head the Office and that the augmentation of the staff of the MDRRMO be made in accordance with the provisions of Section 5.5 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01.
observations.managementAction: The staff designated to perform the "Operations and Warning" functions for the LDRRM Office is a permanent official who now acts on a concurrent capacity.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned Municipal Officials to ensure that the LDRRMF are utilized immediately for the acquisition of the needed equipment and tools and the construction/improvement of facilities, to upgrade the Municipality’s disaster resiliency level and protect vulnerable populace from exposure to the impacts of hazards and disasters.
observations.managementAction: There has an increase in the utilization of the 20% DF from 56% to 66% or P12,895,770.71 to P18,110,210.18 respectively. The Municipality enforced the imposition of penalty to offenders of the "No Segregation, No Collection" which help in gradually institutionalizing the culture of segregation among its constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as head of the LDC ensure that only those PPAs requiring immediate funding and are responsive to the socio-economic needs of the constituents are considered and made part of the investment plan for submission to the SB for approval. We also recommended that the LDC regularly monitor and evaluate the significance of the approved PPAs to the current needs of the constituents, so that those which have become less important and/or no longer responsive to the current situation should be reprogrammed.
observations.managementAction: There has an increase in the utilization of the 20% DF from 56% to 66% or P12,895,770.71 to P18,110,210.18 respectively. However, there is still the need to exert extra effort to ensure that funds intended for developmental projects are fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise all the concerned officials to refund the amount of P15,400.00 as these are paid for personal membership in private organizations, and that all the concerned officials in relation to their participation to private organizations should strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 7160 and NBC No. 563.
observations.managementAction: Expenses incurred by municipal officials in relation to their participation to private organizations were no longer charged to the funds of the Municipality. The P3,000.00 excess registration fee paid by the Municipal Vice Mayor to the VMLP has been drawn as receivables of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure at least 1% of IRA will be appropriated for programs for the prevention, rehabilitation, and reintegration of children at risk and children in conflict with the law.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer explained that the Municipality is operating on limited funds, such that appropriating the entire 1% of the IRA for the LCPC will further reduce the already scarce resources of their town for their regular expenditures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWDO to regularly review and assess the comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention for the determination of its effectiveness and the needs for review and improvement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality enforced the measures and required the MSWDO to submit reports; however, the review and assessment was not completed as of reporting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, strict and adherence to the provisions of the above-cited laws, rules and regulations on the submission of accounts and reports and that the Municipal Mayor ensure timely submission and strict adherence to the prescribed periods.
observations.managementAction: Reports of Disbursements and Collections together with their supporting documents were submitted by the municipality, though made beyond the prescribed time to facilitate the conduct of post audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the OIC-General Services Officer to comply with the provisions of item 7.4 of COA Circular No. 94-006 by notifying the Auditor of all deliveries within the prescribed time to enable the Auditor to conduct timely inspection of deliveries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the required documents though notifications to the Auditor were made late; immediate measures will be enforced to address the observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWD officers engaged in the assessment procedure of the claims for the AICS Program require the applicants to submit complete documentation before issuing the "Certificate of Eligibility" and that the applicants submit the complete supporting documents before the payment is made.
observations.managementAction: Certificates of Eligibility to the applicants were issued only after the applicants have submitted the complete documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the submission of the following documents: a) Official list of all the BHW in the Municipality; b) Official list of the resource speakers designated as such by authorized official; c) Confirmation of the expected participants, resource speakers, and other invited guests; and d) Inventory Custodian Slip for the equipment issued.
observations.managementAction: The required documents were submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 1. Direct the LSB to stop paying honoraria to ALS trainers, and other expenses related to the implementation of the Campus Journalism Act; 2. Direct the LSB not to use the SEF for the expenditures pertaining to the Campus Journalism Act. Subject expense should be charged against the appropriations for DepEd as per Campus Journalism Act of 1991.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2017, the SEF was not used in the implementation of the DEPED’s Campus Journalism Act. Some use of SEF for Campus Journalism was not totally stopped but was limited to a very minimal amount of P12,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise all the concerned officials to refund the amount of P15,400.00 as these are paid for personal membership in private organizations, and that all the concerned officials in relation to their participation to private organizations should strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 7160 and NBC No. 563.
observations.managementAction: Expenses incurred by municipal officials in relation to their participation to private organizations were no longer charged to the funds of the Municipality. The P3,000.00 excess registration fee paid by the Municipal Vice Mayor to the VMLP has been drawn as receivables of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require GAD Focal Point and other Municipal officials concerned to ensure that GAD programs/activities are responsive to the promotion of women empowerment, and gender equality and removal of bias among genders.
